disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The pain came on instantly at the peak of a long run in hot weather with heavy sweating, not gradually over hours or days.
trigger_question_examples: Exactly when did it start — during the run, right at the end, or later?
Did it come on suddenly all at once, or build up gradually?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It hit all at once, right at the end of the run when I was pushing hard. One second I was fine, the next my calf just seized up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Passive stretching gives brief relief and the tight contraction recurs when the leg is rested.
trigger_question_examples: Does anything ease it, even briefly?
What happens when you pull your foot up towards your shin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: If I pull my toes up towards me it loosens for a moment, but as soon as I relax it grips up again.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The hardness is a discrete lump in the muscle belly; the whole leg is not uniformly swollen, red, or hot.
trigger_question_examples: Is the whole leg swollen and red, or is it just one hard spot?
Does the calf feel warmer than the other leg, or look red?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's just the one hard knot in the muscle — the leg overall isn't red or swollen and it doesn't feel hot.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No previous blood clots, no recent long-haul travel, no recent surgery or prolonged immobility.
trigger_question_examples: Have you ever had a blood clot in your leg or lungs?
Any long flights, long drives, surgery, or time in bed recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, never had a clot, no long flights or surgery, and I've been running most days — definitely not sitting still.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Denies chest pain, breathlessness, or coughing up blood.
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain or shortness of breath?
Have you coughed up any blood?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my breathing is fine and my chest feels totally normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: During the prolonged run in the heat the patient sweated heavily and rehydrated mainly with plain water.
trigger_question_examples: How much were you sweating, and what did you drink?
Did you have any electrolytes or just water?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was drenched in sweat the whole run and just kept drinking plain water, no electrolytes or sports drink.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Toes and foot move and feel normal; no numbness, no pins and needles, no coldness of the foot.
trigger_question_examples: Can you feel and wiggle your toes normally?
Is the foot cold, numb, or tingling?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah I can feel and move my toes fine, no numbness or pins and needles, and the foot isn't cold.
